title: Xenial Syrian Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Syrian dish made with lamb, condensed milk, and pickles, cooked in a cataplana vessel, perfect for a midnight craving. This dairy-free-friendly recipe is a unique twist on the classic tagine.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the cataplana vessel over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the sliced pickles and stir to combine.
Pour in the condensed milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
